message:
chore(beads): add new tasks for scheduler integration and GraalVM setup

Add three new tasks to the Beads issue tracker to address emerging work items
identified during recent development cycles. These tasks capture technical debt
and integration work required to complete the scheduler and content extraction
subsystems.

**New Tasks Created**:

ultrasearch-5k6 (Non-Windows disk busy metric source):
- Priority: 2
- Created: 2025-11-21T18:37:53Z
- Dependencies: Blocks c00.4 (Scheduler & background control)
- Scope: Replace stub disk busy detection on non-Windows platforms

Problem statement:
Current implementation uses a placeholder for disk busy detection on Linux/macOS.
Windows implementation uses PDH (Performance Data Helper) for accurate disk I/O
metrics, but non-Windows platforms return hardcoded false.

Required deliverables:
- Real disk busy source using sysinfo per-disk delta calculations
- Platform-specific counters for Linux (/proc/diskstats) and macOS (IOKit)
- Feature-gated implementation with fallback to conservative defaults
- Unit tests for each platform variant
- Configuration options for disk busy threshold tuning

Impact:
- Enables accurate scheduler throttling on non-Windows development machines
- Improves cross-platform testing coverage
- Prevents over-aggressive indexing on busy disks

ultrasearch-5kv (GraalVM provisioning for extractous):
- Priority: 1 (HIGH)
- Created: 2025-11-21T18:37:39Z
- Dependencies: Blocks c00 (Base system epic)
- Scope: Install and configure GraalVM CE 23.x for extractous compilation

Problem statement:
The extractous dependency requires GraalVM for its build.rs compilation step,
which generates JNI bindings to Apache Tika. Currently missing from developer
documentation and CI configuration.

Required deliverables:
- Install GraalVM CE 23.x (compatible with extractous requirements)
- Set JAVA_HOME and PATH environment variables correctly
- Document installation steps in DEVELOPMENT.md
- Verify dockerless local build succeeds on Windows/Linux/macOS
- Add CI environment setup for automated builds
- Document fallback strategy when GraalVM unavailable (disable extractous feature)

Testing requirements:
- Verify extractous_backend feature builds successfully
- Test JNI bridge initialization
- Validate Tika integration with sample documents
- Ensure clean builds without extractous feature enabled

Impact:
- Unblocks content extraction with Extractous backend
- Enables comprehensive document format support (PDF, Office, HTML, etc.)
- Critical for production-ready content indexing

ultrasearch-84j (Wire content job producer into SchedulerRuntime):
- Priority: 1 (HIGH)
- Created: 2025-11-21T18:37:46Z
- Dependencies: Blocks c00.5 (Content extraction & index)
- Scope: Connect NTFS event pipeline to scheduler job queue

Problem statement:
SchedulerRuntime has submit_content_job() API but no production callers.
The USN journal tailer and MFT enumeration produce file events, but these
aren't currently fed into the content indexing pipeline.

Required deliverables:
- Producer component that converts FileMeta events to JobSpec
- Integration in service bootstrap: NTFS watcher → scheduler queue
- DocKey generation with proper volume_id and file_id packing
- Path resolution and validation before submission
- Size limit enforcement (max_bytes, max_chars per job config)
- Queue depth and active worker count propagation to live state
- End-to-end verification: file change → index update

Integration points:
- service/bootstrap.rs: Wire NTFS event channel to scheduler
- SchedulerRuntime: Validate submit_content_job() under real load
- JobDispatcher: Verify worker spawning with real file paths
- index-worker: Test with actual files from MFT/USN pipeline

Testing strategy:
- Create test files on NTFS volume
- Verify USN journal detects changes
- Confirm jobs submitted to scheduler queue
- Validate worker processes files correctly
- Check content index receives updates
- Verify queue/worker metrics accuracy

Success criteria:
- File modifications trigger content indexing within configured latency
- Queue depths accurately reflect pending work
- Worker pool scales based on system load
- No job loss or duplication under concurrent changes
- Proper error handling for inaccessible files

Impact:
- Completes the indexing pipeline end-to-end
- Enables real-time content search capabilities
- Validates scheduler architecture under production workloads

**Task Metadata Updates**:

ultrasearch-c00.9.4 (Adaptive scheduler):
- Updated timestamp: 2025-11-21T17:55:18Z
- Reflects recent refactoring work from stateless to stateful API
- Status remains closed (completed in previous commits)

**Project Context**:

These tasks emerged from:
1. Cross-platform testing revealing Windows-specific metrics
2. Build failures when enabling extractous feature
3. Scheduler refactoring exposing integration gaps

All three tasks are high-priority blockers for their respective epics,
requiring resolution before the corresponding features can be considered
production-ready.

This update maintains accurate task tracking in the multi-agent development
environment, ensuring visibility into emerging technical requirements.
